steps.title: Identify the subject number
steps.content: The subject is "The Proto-Uralic language" — a singular noun. The intervening phrase "common ancestor of thirty-seven Eurasian languages with similar linguistic properties" is an appositive modifier and doesn't change the subject's number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the structure: "The Proto-Uralic language, common ancestor of thirty-seven Eurasian languages..., [BLANK] like all protolanguages, hypothetical." The singular subject requires a singular verb.
  • Choice A — are,: "The Proto-Uralic language...are, like all protolanguages, hypothetical." — "are" is plural; does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice B — were,: "The Proto-Uralic language...were, like all protolanguages, hypothetical." — "were" is plural past tense; does not agree with singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — have been,: "The Proto-Uralic language...have been, like all protolanguages, hypothetical." — "have been" is plural; does not agree with singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — is,: "The Proto-Uralic language...is, like all protolanguages, hypothetical." — "is" is singular present tense; correctly agrees with the singular subject. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
